title: The Dirty Secret Behind AI Agents (Demo 🚀)
excerpt: For quite a while now, I've had the feeling that AI agents are surrounded by this mystical aura....
content_html:
For quite a while now, I've had the feeling that AI agents are surrounded by this mystical aura. Nobody really knows what they're doing, they're probably going to take over the world soon, and if you want to build one yourself... well, obviously you need a framework.

What if I told you that's not true? You can build your own AI agent in about 80 lines of code.

I have to admit, this week hasn't been easy. A lot has been happening at work. On top of that, I received an automatic rejection email for one of my CFPs. Normally, that would have occupied my mind for maybe five minutes, because conference rejections are just part of the game.

Except... I had actually been INVITED to that conference. "You're already accepted, just need to collect the talk details in CFP." Because of that invitation, I turned down two other conference opportunities. Oh well. At least I have September free now.😉

Anyway, life goes on. Today is my birthday, so as a little gift from me to myself (and to all of you!), I wrote this article. 😄 I hope you'll enjoy it!

Do We Really Need a Framework?

Let's get to the point.

Frameworks like LangChain, CrewAI, or Mastra aren't doing magic. They simplify things like conversation memory, tool execution, retries, fallbacks, etc.

Once you understand the underlying mechanism, it becomes much easier to decide when a framework is actually worth using. And even if you end up using one anyway, you'll understand what's happening under the hood.

So I decided to build a small demo and see how little code an AI agent actually needs.

I wrote an AI agent in Node.js in roughly 80 lines of code... okay, okay, the core loop is about 80 lines. There are still tools, a provider abstraction, and a bit of surrounding logic. But come on, "an AI agent in 80 lines" sounds much better. 😄

Here's the repository:

https://github.com/sylwia-lask/code-review-agent

And since it's my birthday... if you enjoy the project, feel free to give it a ⭐. Only if you actually like it, of course. 😄

Meet Steve

The application is a simple code review agent. Well... not exactly.

Meet Steve: a software engineer with 15 years of experience reviewing other people's code. Steve doesn't take anything at face value. Sure, he can be a little sarcastic sometimes... but it's hard to argue with his conclusions.

Here's what one of his code reviews looks like:

Or when you have empty diff:

For now, Steve reviews the local Git diff. Which basically means... he's reviewing himself. So I guess I've built a prototype of the famous self-healing agent that, according to @nitsancohen770, is going to take my job one day.

As you can see, I'm basically helping automate myself out of employment. Maybe it's finally time to start thinking about retirement. 😄

"An Agent Is Just a While Loop"

In my previous article, I joked about people claiming that an AI agent is nothing more than a while loop. Well... Mine isn't even a while loop. It's a for loop, because I wanted to protect myself from accidentally creating an infinite loop and loosing too much money on tokens. 😄

Okay, to be fair, the loop itself doesn't actually do anything. It's simply responsible for orchestrating the process. But the funny part is that most agent frameworks are doing something remarkably similar under the hood.

The loop itself was trivial to write. The real challenge was exactly where you'd expect it to be.

Building an AI agent starts with... choosing an LLM. 😄 This time I picked the Gemini API because tokens for projects like this are ridiculously cheap. I'm definitely tempted to build the next version using a local model, but... hold on. 😄

I immediately ran into one issue: some Gemini models were overloaded, so I kept getting 503 responses. That meant I had to implement something frameworks usually provide out of the box — a simple retry mechanism.

Mine is intentionally very basic. Production frameworks usually offer much more, like exponential backoff, jitter, or automatic fallback to another model.

The next challenge was, of course, writing the right prompt. After that, everything became surprisingly straightforward.

How Does the Agent Actually Work?

It's much simpler than you might think.

Step 1: Send the prompt and available tools

We send two things to the model:

  • the user's message,
  • the list of tools the model is allowed to use.

Here's what the request looks like:

{
  "model": "gemini-2.5-flash",
  "contents": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Please review the current git diff."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "config": {
    "systemInstruction": "You are Steve, a senior software engineer with 15 years of experience...",
    "tools": [
      {
        "functionDeclarations": [
          {
            "name": "getDiff",
            "description": "Get the git diff of the current repository...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {},
              "required": []
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "getFile",
            "description": "Read a file from the repository...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {
                "path": {
                  "type": "STRING",
                  "description": "Path to the file relative to the repository root"
                }
              },
              "required": ["path"]
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "listFiles",
            "description": "List files and directories at a given path...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {
                "path": {
                  "type": "STRING",
                  "description": "Directory path relative to the repository root"
                }
              },
              "required": ["path"]
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

Step 2: Wait for the model's response

The model can respond in one of three ways:

  • with plain text,
  • by requesting a tool call,
  • or with both.

If it returns plain text, we're done: that's our final code review. If it asks us to call a tool, we move on to Step 3.

For example, we might receive:

{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "content": {
        "role": "model",
        "parts": [
          {
            "text": "Let's see what damage we're dealing with today..."
          },
          {
            "functionCall": {
              "id": "call_001",
              "name": "getDiff",
              "args": {}
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}

Step 3: Execute the tool locally

Now it's our application's turn.

We execute the requested tool locally. For example, running git diff or reading a file from the repository, and send the result back to the model as another message in the conversation.

The tools available in this demo are:

  • getDiff
  • getFile
  • listFiles

In other words, everything a good code reviewer needs. 😄

Step 4: Repeat until the model is done

Then we simply go back to Step 2. To avoid getting stuck in an infinite loop, I limit the maximum number of iterations to 10.

There's one more important detail, though.

Take a look at the next request. Notice that we're sending the entire conversation history back to the model:

{
  "contents": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Please review the current git diff."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "role": "model",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Let's see what damage we're dealing with today..."
        },
        {
          "functionCall": {
            "id": "call_001",
            "name": "getDiff",
            "args": {}
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "functionResponse": {
            "id": "call_001",
            "name": "getDiff",
            "response": {
              "result": "diff --git a/src/auth.ts b/src/auth.ts\n--- a/src/auth.ts\n+++ b/src/auth.ts\n@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@\n-  if (password === storedHash) {\n+  if (password == storedHash) {\n"
            }
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

And... that's it. The LLM decides which tool to use and when it's done. Everything else — executing the tools, handling retries, enforcing iteration limits, and orchestrating the loop — is the responsibility of our application.

Simple, isn't it? If you like visualisations, ChatGPT prepared one for us 😉

What's next?

I'd like to write at least two follow-up articles in the future. One about connecting Steve to MCP, and another about replacing the hosted model with a local LLM.

But... hold on. One step at a time. 😄

Bonus: How does the model know it should call a function?

If you only came here to learn how to build an AI agent, you can probably stop reading now. 😄

This section is for the curious ones. Because sooner or later, someone is going to ask: "Sylwia, what are you talking about? You say you're building your own AI agent, but you're just using the Gemini SDK. You send it JSON, you get JSON back."

And, as @darkwiiplayer often points out, an LLM is basically a very sophisticated next-token predictor, not some magical JSON generator.

So... How does the model know what to do when we send it JSON? What would happen if, instead of Gemini, we used some old local Llama model?

Does the Gemini SDK secretly prepend some special prompt? Or are the models themselves trained for this?

The honest answer is that we don't know all the details. What we do know is that models like Gemini and GPT have native support for tool calling. The SDK is responsible for formatting requests correctly and communicating with the API, while the model itself understands tool declarations and can generate function calls when it decides they're needed.

In other words, if we took an older Llama model, we could still write a prompt explaining how to interpret the JSON and asking it to respond in a specific JSON format.

Then we'd simply call JSON.parse()...

...assuming the model actually returns valid JSON instead of Markdown, an explanation, or a few "helpful" comments it decided to add. 😄

That said, it's worth mentioning that newer open-source models increasingly support tool calling natively as well.

Epilogue: An agent written by another agent

Let's be honest. It's 2026. This agent was written largely by... another AI agent. 😄

Since I've been an AWS Community Builder for a while now, I cancelled my Claude Code subscription and switched to Kiro: an AI IDE with built-in support for multiple LLMs, including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. It costs about the same, but thanks to the program, I get to use it for free.

So far, I'm really enjoying it. It feels a bit like an agent-powered layer on top of VS Code, so I immediately felt at home. I also have the feeling that I'm currently using maybe 10% of what it can actually do, so I'm pretty sure I'll write more about it in the future.

Now I'm just hoping AWS will finally send me some swag. 😄 I'm especially dreaming about one of their T-shirts. Years ago, we actually had a political party in Poland called AWS, so I'd absolutely love to see the expressions on some older people's faces. 😄

AWS... if you're reading this... I'm waiting!! 😄

Final Thoughts

As you can see, an AI agent doesn't have to start with a framework. Sometimes all you need is an LLM, tools, convarsation history and a simple loop. Everything else is convenience, production hardening, and quality-of-life improvements.

So...

What do you think about Steve? And how do you like this approach to building an AI agent? 😄

If you liked this post you can also follow me on LinkedIn.

Step 1: Send the prompt and available tools We send two things to the model: the user's message, the list of tools the model is allowed to use. Here's what the request looks like: { "model": "gemini-2.5-flash", "contents": [ { "role": "user", "parts": [ { "text": "Please review the current git diff." } ] } ], "config": { "systemInstruction": "You are Steve, a senior software engineer with 15 years of experience...", "tools": [ { "functionDeclarations": [ { "name": "getDiff", "description": "Get the git diff of the current repository...", "parameters": { "type": "OBJECT", "properties": {}, "required": [] } }, { "name": "getFile", "description": "Read a file from the repository...", "parameters": { "type": "OBJECT", "properties": { "path": { "type": "STRING", "description": "Path to the file relative to the repository root" } }, "required": ["path"] } }, { "name": "listFiles", "description": "List files and directories at a given path...", "parameters": { "type": "OBJECT", "properties": { "path": { "type": "STRING", "description": "Directory path relative to the repository root" } }, "required": ["path"] } } ] } ] } } Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode Step 2: Wait for the model's response The model can respond in one of three ways: with plain text, by requesting a tool call, or with both. blocks.html_en: Meet Steve

sentences.en: Meet Steve
sentences.zh: 认识史蒂夫

blocks.html_en: The application is a simple code review agent. Well... not exactly.

sentences.en: The application is a simple code review agent.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序是一个简单的代码审查代理。

sentences.en: Well...
sentences.zh: 出色地...

sentences.en: not exactly.
sentences.zh: 不完全是。

blocks.html_en: Meet Steve: a software engineer with 15 years of experience reviewing other people's code. Steve doesn't take anything at face value. Sure, he can be a little sarcastic sometimes... but it's hard to argue with his conclusions.

sentences.en: Meet Steve: a software engineer with 15 years of experience reviewing other people's code.
sentences.zh: 认识一下 Steve：一位拥有 15 年审查他人代码经验的软件工程师。

sentences.en: Steve doesn't take anything at face value.
sentences.zh: 史蒂夫不相信任何事情的表面价值。

sentences.en: Sure, he can be a little sarcastic sometimes...
sentences.zh: 当然，他有时会有点讽刺……

sentences.en: but it's hard to argue with his conclusions.
sentences.zh: 但很难反驳他的结论。

blocks.html_en: Here's what one of his code reviews looks like:

sentences.en: Here's what one of his code reviews looks like:
sentences.zh: 这是他的一篇代码审查的样子：

blocks.html_en: Or when you have empty diff:

sentences.en: Or when you have empty diff:
sentences.zh: 或者当你有空 diff 时：

blocks.html_en: For now, Steve reviews the local Git diff. Which basically means... he's reviewing himself. So I guess I've built a prototype of the famous self-healing agent that, according to @nitsancohen770, is going to take my job one day.

sentences.en: For now, Steve reviews the local Git diff.
sentences.zh: 目前，Steve 检查本地 Git diff。

sentences.en: Which basically means...
sentences.zh: 这基本上意味着...

sentences.en: he's reviewing himself.
sentences.zh: 他正在审视自己。

sentences.en: So I guess I've built a prototype of the famous self-healing agent that, according to @nitsancohen770, is going to take my job one day.
sentences.zh: 所以我想我已经构建了一个著名的自我修复代理的原型，根据 @nitsancohen770 的说法，它有一天会取代我的工作。

blocks.html_en: As you can see, I'm basically helping automate myself out of employment. Maybe it's finally time to start thinking about retirement. 😄

sentences.en: As you can see, I'm basically helping automate myself out of employment.
sentences.zh: 正如你所看到的，我基本上是在帮助自己失业。

sentences.en: Maybe it's finally time to start thinking about retirement. 😄
sentences.zh: 也许终于到了开始考虑退休的时候了。 😄

blocks.html_en: "An Agent Is Just a While Loop"

sentences.en: "An Agent Is Just a While Loop"
sentences.zh: “代理只是一个 while 循环”

blocks.html_en: In my previous article, I joked about people claiming that an AI agent is nothing more than a while loop. Well... Mine isn't even a while loop. It's a for loop, because I wanted to protect myself from accidentally creating an infinite loop and loosing too much money on tokens. 😄

sentences.en: In my previous article, I joked about people claiming that an AI agent is nothing more than a while loop.
sentences.zh: 在我之前的文章中，我开玩笑说有人声称人工智能代理只不过是一个 while 循环。

sentences.en: Well...
sentences.zh: 出色地...

sentences.en: Mine isn't even a while loop.
sentences.zh: 我的甚至不是 while 循环。

sentences.en: It's a for loop, because I wanted to protect myself from accidentally creating an infinite loop and loosing too much money on tokens. 😄
sentences.zh: 这是一个 for 循环，因为我想保护自己免于意外创建无限循环并损失太多代币资金。 😄

blocks.html_en: Okay, to be fair, the loop itself doesn't actually do anything. It's simply responsible for orchestrating the process. But the funny part is that most agent frameworks are doing something remarkably similar under the hood.

sentences.en: Okay, to be fair, the loop itself doesn't actually do anything.
sentences.zh: 好吧，公平地说，循环本身实际上并没有做任何事情。

sentences.en: It's simply responsible for orchestrating the process.
sentences.zh: 它只是负责协调该过程。

sentences.en: But the funny part is that most agent frameworks are doing something remarkably similar under the hood.
sentences.zh: 但有趣的是，大多数代理框架在幕后都在做非常相似的事情。

blocks.html_en: The loop itself was trivial to write. The real challenge was exactly where you'd expect it to be.

sentences.en: The loop itself was trivial to write.
sentences.zh: 循环本身编写起来很简单。

sentences.en: The real challenge was exactly where you'd expect it to be.
sentences.zh: 真正的挑战正是您所期望的。

blocks.html_en: Building an AI agent starts with... choosing an LLM. 😄 This time I picked the Gemini API because tokens for projects like this are ridiculously cheap. I'm definitely tempted to build the next version using a local model, but... hold on. 😄

sentences.en: Building an AI agent starts with...
sentences.zh: 构建人工智能代理从......

sentences.en: choosing an LLM. 😄 This time I picked the Gemini API because tokens for projects like this are ridiculously cheap.
sentences.zh: 选择法学硕士。 😄 这次我选择了 Gemini API，因为这样的项目的代币非常便宜。

sentences.en: I'm definitely tempted to build the next version using a local model, but...
sentences.zh: 我绝对想使用本地模型构建下一个版本，但是......

sentences.en: hold on. 😄
sentences.zh: 坚持，稍等。 😄

blocks.html_en: I immediately ran into one issue: some Gemini models were overloaded, so I kept getting 503 responses. That meant I had to implement something frameworks usually provide out of the box — a simple retry mechanism.

sentences.en: I immediately ran into one issue: some Gemini models were overloaded, so I kept getting 503 responses.
sentences.zh: 我立即遇到了一个问题：一些 Gemini 模型超载，所以我不断收到 503 回复。

sentences.en: That meant I had to implement something frameworks usually provide out of the box — a simple retry mechanism.
sentences.zh: 这意味着我必须实现框架通常提供的开箱即用的东西——一个简单的重试机制。

blocks.html_en: Mine is intentionally very basic. Production frameworks usually offer much more, like exponential backoff, jitter, or automatic fallback to another model.

sentences.en: Mine is intentionally very basic.
sentences.zh: 我的设计是故意非常基础的。

sentences.en: Production frameworks usually offer much more, like exponential backoff, jitter, or automatic fallback to another model.
sentences.zh: 生产框架通常提供更多功能，例如指数退避、抖动或自动回退到另一个模型。

blocks.html_en: The next challenge was, of course, writing the right prompt. After that, everything became surprisingly straightforward.

sentences.en: The next challenge was, of course, writing the right prompt.
sentences.zh: 当然，下一个挑战是写出正确的提示。

sentences.en: After that, everything became surprisingly straightforward.
sentences.zh: 之后，一切都变得出奇的简单。

blocks.html_en: How Does the Agent Actually Work?

sentences.en: How Does the Agent Actually Work?
sentences.zh: 代理实际上是如何工作的？

blocks.html_en: It's much simpler than you might think.

sentences.en: It's much simpler than you might think.
sentences.zh: 这比您想象的要简单得多。

blocks.html_en: Step 1: Send the prompt and available tools

sentences.en: Step 1: Send the prompt and available tools
sentences.zh: 第 1 步：发送提示和可用工具

blocks.html_en: We send two things to the model:

sentences.en: We send two things to the model:
sentences.zh: 我们向模型发送两件事：

blocks.html_en: • the user's message,

sentences.en: the user's message,
sentences.zh: 用户的留言，

blocks.html_en: • the list of tools the model is allowed to use.

sentences.en: the list of tools the model is allowed to use.
sentences.zh: 允许模型使用的工具列表。

blocks.html_en: Here's what the request looks like:

sentences.en: Here's what the request looks like:
sentences.zh: 该请求如下所示：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: Step 2: Wait for the model's response

sentences.en: Step 2: Wait for the model's response
sentences.zh: 第2步：等待模型的响应

blocks.html_en: The model can respond in one of three ways:

sentences.en: The model can respond in one of three ways:
sentences.zh: 该模型可以通过以下三种方式之一做出响应：

blocks.html_en: • with plain text,

sentences.en: with plain text,
sentences.zh: 用纯文本，

blocks.html_en: • by requesting a tool call,

sentences.en: by requesting a tool call,
sentences.zh: 通过请求工具调用，

blocks.html_en: • or with both.

sentences.en: or with both.
sentences.zh: 或两者兼而有之。

blocks.html_en: If it returns plain text, we're done: that's our final code review. If it asks us to call a tool, we move on to Step 3.

sentences.en: If it returns plain text, we're done: that's our final code review.
sentences.zh: 如果它返回纯文本，我们就完成了：这是我们的最终代码审查。

sentences.en: If it asks us to call a tool, we move on to Step 3.
sentences.zh: 如果它要求我们调用工具，我们将继续步骤 3。

blocks.html_en: For example, we might receive:

sentences.en: For example, we might receive:
sentences.zh: 例如，我们可能会收到：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: Step 3: Execute the tool locally

sentences.en: Step 3: Execute the tool locally
sentences.zh: 第 3 步：在本地执行该工具

blocks.html_en: Now it's our application's turn.

sentences.en: Now it's our application's turn.
sentences.zh: 现在轮到我们的应用程序了。

blocks.html_en: We execute the requested tool locally. For example, running git diff or reading a file from the repository, and send the result back to the model as another message in the conversation.

sentences.en: We execute the requested tool locally.
sentences.zh: 我们在本地执行请求的工具。

sentences.en: For example, running git diff or reading a file from the repository, and send the result back to the model as another message in the conversation.
sentences.zh: 例如，运行 git diff 或从存储库读取文件，并将结果作为对话中的另一条消息发送回模型。

blocks.html_en: The tools available in this demo are:

sentences.en: The tools available in this demo are:
sentences.zh: 该演示中可用的工具有：

blocks.html_en: • getDiff

sentences.en: getDiff
sentences.zh: 获取差异

blocks.html_en: • getFile

sentences.en: getFile
sentences.zh: 获取文件

blocks.html_en: • listFiles

sentences.en: listFiles
sentences.zh: 列表文件

blocks.html_en: In other words, everything a good code reviewer needs. 😄

sentences.en: In other words, everything a good code reviewer needs. 😄
sentences.zh: 换句话说，一个优秀的代码审查员需要的一切。 😄

blocks.html_en: Step 4: Repeat until the model is done

sentences.en: Step 4: Repeat until the model is done
sentences.zh: 步骤4：重复直到模型完成

blocks.html_en: Then we simply go back to Step 2. To avoid getting stuck in an infinite loop, I limit the maximum number of iterations to 10.

sentences.en: Then we simply go back to Step 2.
sentences.zh: 然后我们只需回到步骤 2。

sentences.en: To avoid getting stuck in an infinite loop, I limit the maximum number of iterations to 10.
sentences.zh: 为了避免陷入无限循环，我将最大迭代次数限制为 10。

blocks.html_en: There's one more important detail, though.

sentences.en: There's one more important detail, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，还有一个更重要的细节。

blocks.html_en: Take a look at the next request. Notice that we're sending the entire conversation history back to the model:

sentences.en: Take a look at the next request.
sentences.zh: 看看下一个请求。

sentences.en: Notice that we're sending the entire conversation history back to the model:
sentences.zh: 请注意，我们将整个对话历史记录发送回模型：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: And... that's it. The LLM decides which tool to use and when it's done. Everything else — executing the tools, handling retries, enforcing iteration limits, and orchestrating the loop — is the responsibility of our application.

sentences.en: And...
that's it.
sentences.zh: 就是这样。

sentences.en: The LLM decides which tool to use and when it's done.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士决定使用哪种工具以及何时完成。

sentences.en: Everything else — executing the tools, handling retries, enforcing iteration limits, and orchestrating the loop — is the responsibility of our application.
sentences.zh: 其他一切 - 执行工具、处理重试、强制迭代限制和编排循环 - 都是我们应用程序的责任。

blocks.html_en: Simple, isn't it? If you like visualisations, ChatGPT prepared one for us 😉

sentences.en: Simple, isn't it?
sentences.zh: 很简单，不是吗？

sentences.en: If you like visualisations, ChatGPT prepared one for us 😉
sentences.zh: 如果您喜欢可视化，ChatGPT 为我们准备了一个 😉

blocks.html_en: What's next?

sentences.en: What's next?
sentences.zh: 接下来怎么办？

blocks.html_en: I'd like to write at least two follow-up articles in the future. One about connecting Steve to MCP, and another about replacing the hosted model with a local LLM.

sentences.en: I'd like to write at least two follow-up articles in the future.
sentences.zh: 我想在未来至少写两篇后续文章。

sentences.en: One about connecting Steve to MCP, and another about replacing the hosted model with a local LLM.
sentences.zh: 一个是关于将 Steve 连接到 MCP，另一个是关于用本地法学硕士替换托管模型。

blocks.html_en: But... hold on. One step at a time. 😄

sentences.en: But...
hold on.
sentences.zh: 坚持，稍等。

sentences.en: One step at a time. 😄
sentences.zh: 一次一步。 😄

blocks.html_en: Bonus: How does the model know it should call a function?

sentences.en: Bonus: How does the model know it should call a function?
sentences.zh: 额外奖励：模型如何知道它应该调用函数？

blocks.html_en: If you only came here to learn how to build an AI agent, you can probably stop reading now. 😄

sentences.en: If you only came here to learn how to build an AI agent, you can probably stop reading now. 😄
sentences.zh: 如果您来这里只是为了学习如何构建人工智能代理，那么您现在可能可以停止阅读。 😄

blocks.html_en: This section is for the curious ones. Because sooner or later, someone is going to ask: "Sylwia, what are you talking about? You say you're building your own AI agent, but you're just using the Gemini SDK. You send it JSON, you get JSON back."

sentences.en: This section is for the curious ones.
sentences.zh: 本节是为好奇的人准备的。

sentences.en: Because sooner or later, someone is going to ask: "Sylwia, what are you talking about?
sentences.zh: 因为迟早有人会问：“西尔维亚，你在说什么？

sentences.en: You say you're building your own AI agent, but you're just using the Gemini SDK.
sentences.zh: 你说你正在构建自己的 AI 代理，但你只是使用 Gemini SDK。

sentences.en: You send it JSON, you get JSON back."
sentences.zh: 你发送 JSON，你就会得到 JSON。”

blocks.html_en: And, as @darkwiiplayer often points out, an LLM is basically a very sophisticated next-token predictor, not some magical JSON generator.

sentences.en: And, as @darkwiiplayer often points out, an LLM is basically a very sophisticated next-token predictor, not some magical JSON generator.
sentences.zh: 而且，正如 @darkwiiplayer 经常指出的那样，LLM 基本上是一个非常复杂的下一个令牌预测器，而不是一些神奇的 JSON 生成器。

blocks.html_en: So... How does the model know what to do when we send it JSON? What would happen if, instead of Gemini, we used some old local Llama model?

sentences.en: So...
sentences.zh: 所以...

sentences.en: How does the model know what to do when we send it JSON?
sentences.zh: 当我们发送 JSON 时，模型如何知道要做什么？

sentences.en: What would happen if, instead of Gemini, we used some old local Llama model?
sentences.zh: 如果我们不使用 Gemini，而是使用一些旧的当地 Llama 模型，会发生什么？

blocks.html_en: Does the Gemini SDK secretly prepend some special prompt? Or are the models themselves trained for this?

sentences.en: Does the Gemini SDK secretly prepend some special prompt?
sentences.zh: Gemini SDK是否暗中前置了一些特殊提示？

sentences.en: Or are the models themselves trained for this?
sentences.zh: 或者模型本身是否为此进行过训练？

blocks.html_en: The honest answer is that we don't know all the details. What we do know is that models like Gemini and GPT have native support for tool calling. The SDK is responsible for formatting requests correctly and communicating with the API, while the model itself understands tool declarations and can generate function calls when it decides they're needed.

sentences.en: The honest answer is that we don't know all the details.
sentences.zh: 诚实的答案是我们不知道所有细节。

sentences.en: What we do know is that models like Gemini and GPT have native support for tool calling.
sentences.zh: 我们所知道的是，像 Gemini 和 GPT 这样的模型本身就支持工具调用。

sentences.en: The SDK is responsible for formatting requests correctly and communicating with the API, while the model itself understands tool declarations and can generate function calls when it decides they're needed.
sentences.zh: SDK 负责正确格式化请求并与 API 进行通信，而模型本身可以理解工具声明，并可以在需要时生成函数调用。

blocks.html_en: In other words, if we took an older Llama model, we could still write a prompt explaining how to interpret the JSON and asking it to respond in a specific JSON format.

sentences.en: In other words, if we took an older Llama model, we could still write a prompt explaining how to interpret the JSON and asking it to respond in a specific JSON format.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，如果我们采用较旧的 Llama 模型，我们仍然可以编写一个提示来解释如何解释 JSON 并要求它以特定的 JSON 格式进行响应。

blocks.html_en: Then we'd simply call JSON.parse()...

sentences.en: Then we'd simply call JSON.parse()...
sentences.zh: 然后我们只需调用 JSON.parse()...

blocks.html_en: ...assuming the model actually returns valid JSON instead of Markdown, an explanation, or a few "helpful" comments it decided to add. 😄

sentences.en: ...assuming the model actually returns valid JSON instead of Markdown, an explanation, or a few "helpful" comments it decided to add. 😄
sentences.zh: ...假设模型实际上返回有效的 JSON 而不是 Markdown、解释或它决定添加的一些“有用”注释。 😄

blocks.html_en: That said, it's worth mentioning that newer open-source models increasingly support tool calling natively as well.

sentences.en: That said, it's worth mentioning that newer open-source models increasingly support tool calling natively as well.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，值得一提的是，较新的开源模型也越来越多地支持本机调用。

blocks.html_en: Epilogue: An agent written by another agent

sentences.en: Epilogue: An agent written by another agent
sentences.zh: 尾声：由另一个特工编写的特工

blocks.html_en: Let's be honest. It's 2026. This agent was written largely by... another AI agent. 😄

sentences.en: Let's be honest.
sentences.zh: 说实话。
现在是 2026 年。

sentences.en: This agent was written largely by...
sentences.zh: 该代理主要是由...编写的

sentences.en: another AI agent. 😄
sentences.zh: 另一个人工智能代理。 😄

blocks.html_en: Since I've been an AWS Community Builder for a while now, I cancelled my Claude Code subscription and switched to Kiro: an AI IDE with built-in support for multiple LLMs, including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. It costs about the same, but thanks to the program, I get to use it for free.

sentences.en: Since I've been an AWS Community Builder for a while now, I cancelled my Claude Code subscription and switched to Kiro: an AI IDE with built-in support for multiple LLMs, including Claude, GPT, and Gemini.
sentences.zh: 由于我已经成为 AWS 社区构建者一段时间了，所以我取消了 Claude Code 订阅并转而使用 Kiro：一个 AI IDE，内置支持多个 LLM，包括 Claude、GPT 和 Gemini。

sentences.en: It costs about the same, but thanks to the program, I get to use it for free.
sentences.zh: 费用大致相同，但由于该程序，我可以免费使用它。

blocks.html_en: So far, I'm really enjoying it. It feels a bit like an agent-powered layer on top of VS Code, so I immediately felt at home. I also have the feeling that I'm currently using maybe 10% of what it can actually do, so I'm pretty sure I'll write more about it in the future.

sentences.en: So far, I'm really enjoying it.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，我真的很享受。

sentences.en: It feels a bit like an agent-powered layer on top of VS Code, so I immediately felt at home.
sentences.zh: 它感觉有点像 VS Code 之上的代理驱动层，所以我立刻就有了宾至如归的感觉。

sentences.en: I also have the feeling that I'm currently using maybe 10% of what it can actually do, so I'm pretty sure I'll write more about it in the future.
sentences.zh: 我还感觉我目前使用的可能只是它实际功能的 10%，所以我很确定我将来会写更多关于它的内容。

blocks.html_en: Now I'm just hoping AWS will finally send me some swag. 😄 I'm especially dreaming about one of their T-shirts. Years ago, we actually had a political party in Poland called AWS, so I'd absolutely love to see the expressions on some older people's faces. 😄

sentences.en: Now I'm just hoping AWS will finally send me some swag. 😄 I'm especially dreaming about one of their T-shirts.
sentences.zh: 现在我只希望 AWS 最终能给我一些礼物。 😄 我特别梦想着他们的一件 T 恤。

sentences.en: Years ago, we actually had a political party in Poland called AWS, so I'd absolutely love to see the expressions on some older people's faces. 😄
sentences.zh: 几年前，我们在波兰实际上有一个名为 AWS 的政党，所以我非常希望看到一些老年人脸上的表情。 😄

blocks.html_en: AWS... if you're reading this... I'm waiting!! 😄

sentences.en: AWS...
sentences.zh: 亚马逊AWS...

sentences.en: if you're reading this...
sentences.zh: 如果你正在读这篇文章...

sentences.en: I'm waiting!! 😄
sentences.zh: 我在等！！ 😄

blocks.html_en: Final Thoughts

sentences.en: Final Thoughts
sentences.zh: 最后的想法

blocks.html_en: As you can see, an AI agent doesn't have to start with a framework. Sometimes all you need is an LLM, tools, convarsation history and a simple loop. Everything else is convenience, production hardening, and quality-of-life improvements.

sentences.en: As you can see, an AI agent doesn't have to start with a framework.
sentences.zh: 正如您所看到的，人工智能代理不必从框架开始。

sentences.en: Sometimes all you need is an LLM, tools, convarsation history and a simple loop.
sentences.zh: 有时，您所需要的只是法学硕士、工具、对话历史记录和简单的循环。

sentences.en: Everything else is convenience, production hardening, and quality-of-life improvements.
sentences.zh: 其他一切都是便利、生产强化和生活质量的改善。

blocks.html_en: What do you think about Steve? And how do you like this approach to building an AI agent? 😄

sentences.en: What do you think about Steve?
sentences.zh: 你觉得史蒂夫怎么样？

sentences.en: And how do you like this approach to building an AI agent? 😄
sentences.zh: 您觉得这种构建人工智能代理的方法怎么样？ 😄

blocks.html_en: If you liked this post you can also follow me on LinkedIn.

sentences.en: If you liked this post you can also follow me on LinkedIn.
sentences.zh: 如果您喜欢这篇文章，也可以在 LinkedIn 上关注我。

content_html_zh:
很长一段时间以来，我一直有一种感觉，人工智能特工被这种神秘的光环包围着。 没有人真正知道他们在做什么，他们可能很快就会接管世界，如果你想自己建造一个...... 嗯，显然你需要一个框架。

如果我告诉你那不是真的怎么办？ 您可以用大约 80 行代码构建自己的 AI 代理。

我必须承认，这周并不轻松。 工作中发生了很多事情。 最重要的是，我收到了一封自动拒绝我的一位 CFP 的电子邮件。 通常情况下，这会占据我的注意力大约五分钟，因为会议拒绝只是游戏的一部分。

除了... 我实际上被邀请参加那个会议。 “你已经被录取了，只需要在CFP中收集谈话详情即可。” 由于这个邀请，我拒绝了另外两次会议机会。 那好吧。 至少我现在有九月空闲了。😉

不管怎样，生活还要继续。 今天是我的生日，所以作为我给自己（以及你们所有人！）的小礼物，我写了这篇文章。 😄 我希望你会喜欢它！

我们真的需要一个框架吗？

让我们进入正题吧。

像 LangChain、CrewAI 或 Mastra 这样的框架并没有发挥神奇作用。 它们简化了对话记忆、工具执行、重试、回退等事情。

一旦了解了底层机制，就可以更轻松地决定框架何时真正值得使用。 即使您最终使用了其中一个，您也会了解幕后发生的情况。

因此，我决定构建一个小型演示，看看人工智能代理实际需要的代码有多少。

我在 Node.js 中用大约 80 行代码编写了一个 AI 代理...... 好吧好吧，核心循环大约80行。 仍然有工具、提供者抽象和一些周围的逻辑。 但拜托，“80 行人工智能代理”听起来好多了。 😄

这是存储库：

https://github.com/sylwia-lask/code-review-agent

而且因为今天是我的生日... 如果您喜欢这个项目，请随意给它一个⭐。 当然，前提是你真的喜欢它。 😄

认识史蒂夫

该应用程序是一个简单的代码审查代理。 出色地... 不完全是。

认识一下 Steve：一位拥有 15 年审查他人代码经验的软件工程师。 史蒂夫不相信任何事情的表面价值。 当然，他有时会有点讽刺…… 但很难反驳他的结论。

这是他的一篇代码审查的样子：

或者当你有空 diff 时：

目前，Steve 检查本地 Git diff。 这基本上意味着... 他正在审视自己。 所以我想我已经构建了一个著名的自我修复代理的原型，根据 @nitsancohen770 的说法，它有一天会取代我的工作。

正如你所看到的，我基本上是在帮助自己失业。 也许终于到了开始考虑退休的时候了。 😄

“代理只是一个 while 循环”

在我之前的文章中，我开玩笑说有人声称人工智能代理只不过是一个 while 循环。 出色地... 我的甚至不是 while 循环。 这是一个 for 循环，因为我想保护自己免于意外创建无限循环并损失太多代币资金。 😄

好吧，公平地说，循环本身实际上并没有做任何事情。 它只是负责协调该过程。 但有趣的是，大多数代理框架在幕后都在做非常相似的事情。

循环本身编写起来很简单。 真正的挑战正是您所期望的。

构建人工智能代理从...... 选择法学硕士。 😄 这次我选择了 Gemini API，因为这样的项目的代币非常便宜。 我绝对想使用本地模型构建下一个版本，但是...... 坚持，稍等。 😄

我立即遇到了一个问题：一些 Gemini 模型超载，所以我不断收到 503 回复。 这意味着我必须实现框架通常提供的开箱即用的东西——一个简单的重试机制。

我的设计是故意非常基础的。 生产框架通常提供更多功能，例如指数退避、抖动或自动回退到另一个模型。

当然，下一个挑战是写出正确的提示。 之后，一切都变得出奇的简单。

代理实际上是如何工作的？

这比您想象的要简单得多。

第 1 步：发送提示和可用工具

我们向模型发送两件事：

  • 用户的留言，
  • 允许模型使用的工具列表。

该请求如下所示：

{
  "model": "gemini-2.5-flash",
  "contents": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Please review the current git diff."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "config": {
    "systemInstruction": "You are Steve, a senior software engineer with 15 years of experience...",
    "tools": [
      {
        "functionDeclarations": [
          {
            "name": "getDiff",
            "description": "Get the git diff of the current repository...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {},
              "required": []
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "getFile",
            "description": "Read a file from the repository...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {
                "path": {
                  "type": "STRING",
                  "description": "Path to the file relative to the repository root"
                }
              },
              "required": ["path"]
            }
          },
          {
            "name": "listFiles",
            "description": "List files and directories at a given path...",
            "parameters": {
              "type": "OBJECT",
              "properties": {
                "path": {
                  "type": "STRING",
                  "description": "Directory path relative to the repository root"
                }
              },
              "required": ["path"]
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

第2步：等待模型的响应

该模型可以通过以下三种方式之一做出响应：

  • 用纯文本，
  • 通过请求工具调用，
  • 或两者兼而有之。

如果它返回纯文本，我们就完成了：这是我们的最终代码审查。 如果它要求我们调用工具，我们将继续步骤 3。

例如，我们可能会收到：

{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "content": {
        "role": "model",
        "parts": [
          {
            "text": "Let's see what damage we're dealing with today..."
          },
          {
            "functionCall": {
              "id": "call_001",
              "name": "getDiff",
              "args": {}
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

第 3 步：在本地执行该工具

现在轮到我们的应用程序了。

我们在本地执行请求的工具。 例如，运行 git diff 或从存储库读取文件，并将结果作为对话中的另一条消息发送回模型。

该演示中可用的工具有：

  • 获取差异
  • 获取文件
  • 列表文件

换句话说，一个优秀的代码审查员需要的一切。 😄

步骤4：重复直到模型完成

然后我们只需回到步骤 2。 为了避免陷入无限循环，我将最大迭代次数限制为 10。

不过，还有一个更重要的细节。

看看下一个请求。 请注意，我们将整个对话历史记录发送回模型：

{
  "contents": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Please review the current git diff."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "role": "model",
      "parts": [
        {
          "text": "Let's see what damage we're dealing with today..."
        },
        {
          "functionCall": {
            "id": "call_001",
            "name": "getDiff",
            "args": {}
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "parts": [
        {
          "functionResponse": {
            "id": "call_001",
            "name": "getDiff",
            "response": {
              "result": "diff --git a/src/auth.ts b/src/auth.ts\n--- a/src/auth.ts\n+++ b/src/auth.ts\n@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@\n-  if (password === storedHash) {\n+  if (password == storedHash) {\n"
            }
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

和... 就是这样。 法学硕士决定使用哪种工具以及何时完成。 其他一切 - 执行工具、处理重试、强制迭代限制和编排循环 - 都是我们应用程序的责任。

很简单，不是吗？ 如果您喜欢可视化，ChatGPT 为我们准备了一个 😉

接下来怎么办？

我想在未来至少写两篇后续文章。 一个是关于将 Steve 连接到 MCP，另一个是关于用本地法学硕士替换托管模型。

但... 坚持，稍等。 一次一步。 😄

额外奖励：模型如何知道它应该调用函数？

如果您来这里只是为了学习如何构建人工智能代理，那么您现在可能可以停止阅读。 😄

本节是为好奇的人准备的。 因为迟早有人会问：“西尔维亚，你在说什么？ 你说你正在构建自己的 AI 代理，但你只是使用 Gemini SDK。 你发送 JSON，你就会得到 JSON。”

而且，正如 @darkwiiplayer 经常指出的那样，LLM 基本上是一个非常复杂的下一个令牌预测器，而不是一些神奇的 JSON 生成器。

所以... 当我们发送 JSON 时，模型如何知道要做什么？ 如果我们不使用 Gemini，而是使用一些旧的当地 Llama 模型，会发生什么？

Gemini SDK是否暗中前置了一些特殊提示？ 或者模型本身是否为此进行过训练？

诚实的答案是我们不知道所有细节。 我们所知道的是，像 Gemini 和 GPT 这样的模型本身就支持工具调用。 SDK 负责正确格式化请求并与 API 进行通信，而模型本身可以理解工具声明，并可以在需要时生成函数调用。

换句话说，如果我们采用较旧的 Llama 模型，我们仍然可以编写一个提示来解释如何解释 JSON 并要求它以特定的 JSON 格式进行响应。

然后我们只需调用 JSON.parse()...

...假设模型实际上返回有效的 JSON 而不是 Markdown、解释或它决定添加的一些“有用”注释。 😄

也就是说，值得一提的是，较新的开源模型也越来越多地支持本机调用。

尾声：由另一个特工编写的特工

说实话。 现在是 2026 年。 该代理主要是由...编写的 另一个人工智能代理。 😄

由于我已经成为 AWS 社区构建者一段时间了，所以我取消了 Claude Code 订阅并转而使用 Kiro：一个 AI IDE，内置支持多个 LLM，包括 Claude、GPT 和 Gemini。 费用大致相同，但由于该程序，我可以免费使用它。

到目前为止，我真的很享受。 它感觉有点像 VS Code 之上的代理驱动层，所以我立刻就有了宾至如归的感觉。 我还感觉我目前使用的可能只是它实际功能的 10%，所以我很确定我将来会写更多关于它的内容。

现在我只希望 AWS 最终能给我一些礼物。 😄 我特别梦想着他们的一件 T 恤。 几年前，我们在波兰实际上有一个名为 AWS 的政党，所以我非常希望看到一些老年人脸上的表情。 😄

亚马逊AWS... 如果你正在读这篇文章... 我在等！！ 😄

最后的想法

正如您所看到的，人工智能代理不必从框架开始。 有时，您所需要的只是法学硕士、工具、对话历史记录和简单的循环。 其他一切都是便利、生产强化和生活质量的改善。

So...

你觉得史蒂夫怎么样？ 您觉得这种构建人工智能代理的方法怎么样？ 😄

如果您喜欢这篇文章，也可以在 LinkedIn 上关注我。
